---
title: "Appendices — Working references"
description: "Mathematical and engineering-practice references used throughout the book."
page-role: part
part-id: appendices
---

These appendices support the main text at the moment a method is needed. They
are not remedial side material and do not need to be read from beginning to end
before starting electronics.

## Two reference threads

| Thread | Appendices | Use |
|---|---|---|
| Mathematical tools | M01–M03 | units and algebra; dynamic and linear models; uncertainty, statistics, and numerical methods |
| Engineering practice | T01–T03 | evaluate evidence; manage tools and versions; create reproducible records and releases |

## How to use them

Follow a prerequisite link when a chapter introduces an unfamiliar technique.
Work enough examples to use the technique and interpret its limits, then return
to the engineering question. Revisit the appendix when the consequence of error
increases—for example, when an estimate becomes a calibration claim or a local
file becomes a release artifact.

The appendices share the book’s evidence standard: define quantities, expose
assumptions, preserve recoverable work, and connect each calculation or record
to the decision it supports.
